title: Behavioral Grounding Framework (BGF): LLM-Grounded Multi-Agent Economic Simulation
description: BGF is an open-source multi-agent economic simulation framework that grounds LLM-based agents in empirical socio-economic microdata from the European Social Survey (ESS Round 11). It provides: (i) a formal framework with closed-form bounds on RLHF cooperative bias (Proposition 1: B_RLHF <= 1 - 1/|A|); (ii) the Behavioral Realism Metric (BRM) and RLHF Bias Index (B_RLHF) as evaluation primitives; (iii) a deterministic rule-based ESS policy (Condition D) that reproduces Eurostat-range Gini (0.325, BCa 95% CI [0.324, 0.325] at N=500, T=30, 10 seeds) and a perfect cross-cultural cooperation gradient (Spearman rho = +1.000 vs ESS-11 trust, replicated against WVS Wave 7 r = +0.977 and Herrmann-Thoeni-Gaechter 2008 PGG contributions rho = +0.886, p = 0.033); (iv) an LLM policy stack with dual SQL/Graph RAG, hierarchical memory, and anti-hallucination output parsing. The pre-registered 10-seed N=100 confirmatory extension finds the within-Mistral-7B grounded vs ungrounded contrast statistically null (MWU p > 0.85). At N=500 (T=30 complete, 2026-06-05), a self-amplifying RLHF cooperation cascade drives both conditions to 94-96% cooperation with B_RLHF 0.607/0.627 at terminal (2.6-2.8x the N=100 baseline). Memory ablation H8 (24/24 cells) is falsified for both arms. Ships with 1,578 automated tests.
notes: Reproduces with `bash scripts/reproduce_paper.sh` (CPU-only baselines, ~30 min) or `--figures` (figure-regen only). GPU-dependent LLM experiments require a 16 GB CUDA device (Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 at 4-bit quantization) and are documented in scripts/pipeline_*.sh. 1,578 automated tests passing (2026-06-05). Pinned dependencies in requirements.txt. Container build: see Dockerfile.
